methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Apis Cor is a seed-stage construction-technology company that has raised $5.5M to date. The Housing & Construction sector contains a sparse comparable universe; two private peers (ICON and BotBuilt) are identifiable, though neither meets the criteria for a statistically reliable peer-median valuation due to stage and valuation-tier mismatches. No public comparables exist in this narrow domain.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total raised ($5.5M, rounded to $6M as a proxy for post-money valuation) as the base, a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to Series A yields an implied valuation of $14M. This single-method triangulation produces a range of $12M (low) to $16M (high), with a median point estimate of $14M.

The analysis supports a valuation in the $12–16M range, centered at $14M. Key uncertainties stem from the absence of granular peer comps; the stage-progression multiplier relies on sector-agnostic benchmarks rather than Housing & Construction-specific progression patterns. Any upside or downside sensitivity hinges on the company's progress toward demonstrable unit economics, market traction, and capital efficiency relative to peers in adjacent construction-tech segments. Additional funding rounds or comparable transactions in the sector would materially refine this estimate.
